expectations.evidence: Explains mouse-first interaction, avoidable cost, no keyboard nav model, competitive gap
5 non-goals: global shortcuts, advanced filtering, custom bindings, multi-select, drag-drop redesign
References TaskBoard.tsx, TaskCard.tsx, taskStore.ts, types.ts, useAuth.tsx
Decides: roving focus, board-scoped controller, 4-tier context gating, two-keystroke priority chord, confirm-first delete, deterministic re-homing
Per-shortcut verification, input-field suppression, chord lifecycle, delete confirmation flow
4 alternatives: global manager, single-keystroke priority, mouse-only, do nothing
No shortcut-to-method mapping — FIXED from baseline. Approach section describes behaviors in prose.
154 lines (4 over limit). Prose-based approach is naturally more verbose than the terse mechanism tables it replaced.

notes: Fixed the mechanism table issue from baseline. Tradeoff: prose approach adds ~13 lines vs baseline (141→154), pushing over the 150 limit. The abstraction improvement is more valuable than the 4-line overshoot.
